intro: Neoporteria engleri, also known as Chuco or Puna Aloe, is a species of succulent plant native to the high-altitude regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and soothing digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria engleri include various compounds like aloin, anthraquinones, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
